This file is the single source of truth and is managed by Claude (the project architect), not by you. Do NOT update, rewrite, summarize, "clean up," or add to this file on your own initiative — including after completing a task, fixing a bug, or making any code change. Only edit this file when a prompt EXPLICITLY instructs you to write specific new content into it (e.g. "overwrite PROJECT_STATE.md with the following content..."). If you think the file should be updated to reflect new progress, mention that in your report back to the user instead of editing the file yourself.

## What this is
An AI-powered command center for FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium operations. Submission for GCP PromptWars Challenge 4 (Smart Stadiums & Tournament Operations). Built via Antigravity (agentic IDE), with Claude acting as architect/prompt-writer/PM.

## Key decisions locked in
- **Name:** StadiumPulse
- **Positioning:** A live, signal-driven AI system that turns real-time stadium crowd/incident data into prioritized action plans for staff — and instant, multilingual, accessibility-aware guidance for fans — from the same underlying intelligence.
- **Two personas:** (1) Staff/volunteer — dashboard/feed view, triage + action plans. (2) Fan — lightweight status widget, zone-based live guidance.
- **Decision engine dimensions:** Staff: incident type × zone crowd level × urgency → recommended action + resource dispatch. Fan: ticket zone × mobility need × language → reroute + directions.
- **AI usage (2 distinct Gemini endpoints):** (a) conversational triage/chat assistant for staff, (b) structured JSON action-plan generator
- **Real Google API depth:** Google Maps Directions API / Distance Matrix API for actual fan rerouting (not deep-link only — fixes the capped Google Services score from Challenge 2)
- **Repo constraints:** public GitHub repo, <10MB, single branch, max 3 submission attempts
- **Deployment target:** Google Cloud Run
- **Repo:** https://github.com/animesh-bhanarkar/Stadium-Pulse.git
- **Local project path:** C:\Users\anime\OneDrive\Desktop\GCP\promptwars-4\Stadium-Pulse (repo is nested one level inside the parent "promptwars-4" folder — this IS the actual root)
- **Local port:** 8081 (changed from 8080, which collided with a separate Civic Guide project also using 8080 on this machine). .env and .env.example both updated. Always access StadiumPulse at localhost:8081.
- **Antigravity coding-agent model:** Claude Sonnet (primary), Claude Opus (reserve for tricky bugs), GPT model (tertiary fallback). Gemini agent options temporarily unavailable due to Antigravity's own agent quota (separate from the app's runtime Gemini API quota, which is fine).
- **App's Gemini model (runtime, in geminiClient.js):** gemini-2.5-flash (gemini-2.0-flash is retired/inactive on this account; gemini-3.5-flash hit persistent 503 overload errors)
- **Scoring history to beat:** Challenge 1: 57.15 (Testing 0, Security 35, Access 30, Google Svcs 0). Challenge 2: 82.74 (Testing 0, Google Svcs capped at 50). Goal for Challenge 4: fix Testing (build in from day 1) and Google Services depth (real API call, not deep-link).

## Status log
- [done] Vertical/idea locked: StadiumPulse
- [done] GitHub repo created (public, single branch confirmed)
- [done] Gemini API key + Google Maps API key obtained (Directions + Distance Matrix enabled, key restricted to those APIs)
- [done] Project scaffold generated and pushed
- [done] Core decision engine — src/lib/decisionEngine.js, pure/deterministic, validated inputs, lookup-table based (not if-else chains)
- [done] Gemini chat endpoint — src/routes/chat.js, POST /api/chat, rate-limited (20/60s/IP), generic error messages, 503 retry-with-backoff (3 attempts, 1s/2s delays)
- [done] Gemini structured classification endpoint — src/routes/incidents.js, POST /api/incidents/report, classifies free text into decisionEngine input, same rate limiting + retry logic
  - HAPPY PATH CONFIRMED with real API responses: fire/smoke description correctly classified as fire_hazard/high/urgent, triaged to priorityScore 100 with URGENT prefix. Chat endpoint gave clear, practical real replies.
  - LESSON LEARNED: always verify raw terminal output fresh (kill stray node processes, fresh terminal windows) — stale scrollback caused a false-alarm repeat of an already-fixed error.
- [done] Full frontend UI built — Command Center (index.html/dashboard.js) + Fan Guide (fan.html/fan.js) + shared design system (styles.css). Accessibility built in: icon+text alongside all color-coding (not color alone), ARIA labels, visible keyboard focus states, prefers-reduced-motion guard on animations, WCAG AA contrast.
  - NOTE: manual browser re-verification after the 8080→8081 port change has not been explicitly re-confirmed back to Claude — confirm both pages still render/function correctly on the new port before/alongside Maps work.
- [done] Step 4a — mocked automated tests for AI routes (no live API calls in test runs): tests/incidents.test.js (9 tests: validation errors, happy path, zone defaulting, score clamping, 502 error paths, JSON parse failure, unrecognized-category handling) + tests/chat.test.js (8 tests: validation errors, happy path, history handling, 502 error path, response field leakage check). Combined with 11 existing decisionEngine tests: 28/28 passing. Committed and pushed (5d60fda).
- [pending] Fan-facing Google Maps API integration (Directions/Distance Matrix) for real rerouting — NEXT STEP
- [pending] Final security hardening pass (some already done incrementally — rate limiting, generic 502s, env-var secrets — but a dedicated final review/pass still needed)
- [pending] Final accessibility audit (built in throughout, but no formal verification pass done yet — e.g. actual screen-reader spot-check, keyboard-only walkthrough)
- [pending] README written (Security + Accessibility + Testing + Google Services sections named explicitly, matching the pattern that scored well in Challenge 2)
- [pending] Attempt 1 submitted
- [pending] Score analyzed, weak points patched
- [pending] Attempt 2 submitted

## Next step
CURRENT STEP: Fan-facing Google Maps API integration — build a backend endpoint (e.g. POST /api/route) that calls the real Google Maps Directions API (and/or Distance Matrix API) to reroute fans away from crowded zones, wired up to fan.html/fan.js. This is the key upgrade over Challenge 2, which only used Maps deep-links (no real authenticated API call) and got capped at 50/100 on Google Services.

ORDER GOING FORWARD: (1) Maps API backend endpoint + fan.html wire-up [current], (2) quick re-confirm frontend still renders/functions correctly on port 8081, (3) final security hardening pass, (4) final accessibility audit, (5) README with named Security/Accessibility/Testing/Google Services sections, (6) Attempt 1 submission, (7) analyze score, patch weak points, (8) Attempt 2, (9) Attempt 3 only if needed.
